title: ds-viz-bubble - option matrix (dark)
description: Test bench for splunk.bubble. Verifies all options from the 10.4 PDF: x/y/size/category DOS bindings, bubbleSizeMin/Max/Method (area|diameter), seriesColors, seriesColorsByField, axis surface (Min/Max, Scale linear|log, Abbreviation, Title/Label/Line/Tick visibility, gridlines), legend, backgroundColor, resultLimit. Pairs with the canonical splunkd_access example from the PDF.

options.query: | makeresults count=20 | streamstats count AS rn | eval x_val = round(10 + random()%90) | eval y_val = round(20 + random()%180) | eval size_val = round(100 + random()%900) | table x_val y_val size_val

ds_four_dim.name: four-dim with category - latency vs throughput, size=requests, color=service

options.query: | makeresults count=40 | streamstats count AS rn | eval service = case(rn%4==0,"api", rn%4==1,"db", rn%4==2,"cache", 1==1,"queue") | eval throughput = case(service=="api",100+random()%400, service=="db",50+random()%200, service=="cache",500+random()%1500, 1==1,200+random()%600) | eval latency = case(service=="api",20+random()%80, service=="db",100+random()%300, service=="cache",5+random()%20, 1==1,40+random()%120) | eval requests = round(1000 + random()%9000) | table throughput latency requests service

ds_canonical_pdf.name: canonical PDF example - status x date_hour x count x total_bytes

options.query: | makeresults count=72 | streamstats count AS rn | eval date_hour = (rn-1)%24 | eval status = case(rn%4==0,"200", rn%4==1,"301", rn%4==2,"404", 1==1,"500") | eval count = case(status=="200",100+random()%400, status=="301",10+random()%30, status=="404",5+random()%50, 1==1,1+random()%10) | eval total_bytes = count * (500 + random()%2000) | stats sum(count) AS count sum(total_bytes) AS total_bytes by status, date_hour | table date_hour count total_bytes status

ds_log_scale.name: wide-range bubble data - candidates for log scale

options.query: | makeresults count=30 | streamstats count AS rn | eval x_val = rn | eval y_val = pow(2, rn%10) + random()%50 | eval size_val = round(50 + random()%450) | table x_val y_val size_val

options.markdown:
# `splunk.bubble` - option matrix

Bubble = scatter + a third numeric dimension encoded as marker size. Optional fourth dimension via `category` colors.

Verified options from the **10.4 PDF**:
- DOS bindings: `x`, `y`, `size`, `category`
- Field labels: `xField`, `yField`, `sizeField`, `categoryField`
- Sizing: `bubbleSizeMin` (10), `bubbleSizeMax` (50), `bubbleSizeMethod` (`area` | `diameter`)
- `resultLimit` (50000), `backgroundColor`
- Color: `seriesColors`, `seriesColorsByField`
- Full axis surface (Min/Max, Scale linear|log, Abbreviation, Title/Label/Line/Tick visibility, MajorTickInterval, ExtendedRange, WithZero, MinorGridLines)
- Legend: `legendDisplay`, `legendTruncation`

viz_default.title: 1. Default - x, y, size from columns 0/1/2
viz_default.description: No options. Bound by series order. Single color since no category.

viz_explicit_named.title: 2. Explicit bindings by name
viz_explicit_named.description: x/y/size pinned via seriesByName. Adds axis titles.

viz_with_category.title: 3. Four-dim with category
viz_with_category.description: x=throughput, y=latency, size=requests, category=service. Each service gets its own color.

viz_size_max_big.title: 4. bubbleSizeMax 100
viz_size_max_big.description: Default cap is 50. Bigger cap = more visual weight on largest values.

viz_size_method_diameter.title: 6. bubbleSizeMethod=diameter
viz_size_method_diameter.description: Default is 'area' (preserves visual proportion). 'diameter' makes large values look even larger.

viz_custom_palette.title: 7. Custom seriesColors
viz_custom_palette.description: Brand palette overrides default. Maps in alphabetical category order.

viz_colors_by_field.title: 8. seriesColorsByField
viz_colors_by_field.description: Lock specific colors to category values regardless of order.

viz_log_y.title: 9. Log scale on y
viz_log_y.description: yAxisScale=log. y_val spans several orders of magnitude.

viz_axes_clean.title: 10. Stripped editorial style
viz_axes_clean.description: Titles + axis lines + grids off. Pure point cloud.

viz_canonical_pdf.title: 12. Canonical PDF example - status x date_hour
viz_canonical_pdf.description: Reproduces the splunkd_access example: x=date_hour, y=count, size=total_bytes, category=status.

viz_tinted.title: 13. backgroundColor tint
viz_tinted.description: Custom panel background paired with brand palette.
